response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically strong individual contributor with 15+ years of experience and genuine exposure to developer tooling, test frameworks, and CI/CD infrastructure. However, the role is explicitly a full-time people-leadership position requiring 2+ years of people management, and his resume shows no evidence of direct reports, performance reviews, or team-building responsibilities. His Staff-level roles and workshop facilitation demonstrate leadership inclination, but that does not substitute for the managerial competencies this position demands. Without a people management track record, he is not a viable candidate regardless of technical depth.
strengths: Deep developer tooling experience: built test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), configured CI/CD pipelines, and authored MCP code generators — directly aligned with the engineering productivity focus
Broad polyglot technical depth spanning web, mobile (iOS/Apple TV), embedded, and distributed systems, which supports the system design and architecture responsibilities
Teaching and knowledge-sharing track record via Selenium courses, workshops, and conference talks demonstrates communication skills and an engineering culture mindset
concerns: Zero evidence of people management: no mention of direct reports, hiring, performance feedback cycles, or organizational ownership at any point in a 15-year career
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, owning roadmaps at an org level, or driving strategic change — all core to this L2 manager role
Mobile platform knowledge is shallow and dated (iOS/Apple TV work ended in 2016), which is a gap for a role explicitly centered on a major mobile platform ecosystem

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and test infrastructure expertise
Absence of organizational leadership or roadmap ownership
